@@ -1339,7 +1339,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","title":"Alien Artifact: queueing-theoretic backpressure + fairness policy (remote protocol)","description":"Goal
- Design backpressure/flow-control for the websocket protocol so we never OOM and we maintain low interactive latency under load.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Queueing model for input/output queues (arrival/service rates, tail latency).
- Explicit objective: minimize expected loss with an asymmetric loss matrix:
  - huge penalty for OOM/unbounded memory
  - large penalty for keystroke latency spikes
  - smaller penalty for throughput loss
- Use an evidence ledger: record measured rates, chosen thresholds, and why.

Deliverables
- A spec for bounded queues + drop/merge policy (e.g. coalesce resize/mouse-move events, chunk outputs).
- A fairness policy so long outputs don't starve input (interactive priority).

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress harness demonstrates bounded memory and stable p95 keystroke latency under worst-case output floods.
- Policy is explainable: given metrics, we can show why a drop/coalesce happened.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2","title":"FrankenTerm Web: WebGPU renderer + web input plumbing","description":"Scope
- Build `frankenterm-web`: a WebGPU-first terminal renderer + input stack compiled to WASM (no xterm.js).

User-Facing Requirements
- Crisp text + stable geometry under resize/zoom/DPR changes.
- Smooth scrollback (virtualized rendering; no O(n) over history per frame).
- Selection + copy/paste, OSC 8 hyperlinks (hover/click), and in-terminal search.
- Accessibility strategy despite canvas/WebGPU rendering (DOM mirror mode, focus, high-contrast, reduced motion).

Deliverables
- `frankenterm-web` crate exporting a minimal JS/WASM API: init, resize, input, render/present.
- WebGPU renderer (glyph atlas + instancing) with deterministic patch application.
- Input capture: keyboard (modifiers/repeat), mouse + wheel, touch; clipboard; IME/composition.
- Bench + E2E harnesses emitting detailed JSONL for correctness + perf gates.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:03.657124887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","title":"ADR: FrankenTerm WebGPU renderer architecture (glyph atlas, batching, present)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the renderer design for frankenterm-web.

Must Cover
- Rendering primitive set: background rects, text glyph quads, underline/strikethrough, cursor, selection.
- Glyph pipeline: bitmap vs SDF/MSDF; atlas packing; eviction; metrics.
- Batching: instancing strategy, buffer update strategy (full vs dirty spans), CPU->GPU transfer budgets.
- Color management: sRGB, blending rules for selections/cursor.

Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer tasks can be implemented without ambiguity.
- Includes a measurable frame budget and how we will profile it.
","notes":"Drafted ADR-009 at docs/adr/ADR-009-webgpu-renderer-architecture.md (WebGPU-first, glyph atlas, instanced quads, patch-driven updates, determinism gates via patch/frame hashes).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:30.896274992Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:30.896256127Z","close_reason":"Completed: ADR-009 WebGPU renderer architecture","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","title":"Bench: web renderer frame-time harness + regression thresholds","description":"Goal
- Build a repeatable web performance harness for FrankenTerm that supports the Extreme Optimization loop.

Metrics (minimum)
- Frame time histogram (p50/p95/p99) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Time-to-first-frame and time-to-interactive.
- Patch statistics: bytes per frame, dirty-span counts, draw-call counts.
- Memory usage (JS + WASM) steady-state and after stress (scrollback/resize).
- GPU timings when available (best-effort).

Outputs
- Machine-readable JSON for CI gating and local profiling.
- Optional JSONL stream for per-frame/per-event diagnostics (run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR).

Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline runs are stable enough to detect regressions (document expected variance + thresholds).
- Harness can be driven by deterministic traces (record/replay) so profiles are comparable.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.11","title":"Optional: Canvas2D fallback renderer","description":"Goal
- Provide a fallback renderer for environments without WebGPU.

Acceptance Criteria
- Same correctness semantics; perf can be lower but still usable for demos.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","title":"Optimize: WebGPU renderer hotspot matrix + one-lever passes","description":"Goal
- Apply the Extreme Optimization workflow to the web renderer.

Process
- Profile (CPU + GPU) with the harness.
- Build an opportunity matrix; implement only Score >= 2.0.
- For each change: isomorphism proof + golden checks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Documented perf wins with before/after p95 frame times.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.13","title":"Implement: fit-to-container + DPI scaling + font metrics/zoom","description":"Goal
- Make FrankenTerm feel native in the browser: correct cell geometry, crisp text, and stable layout under resize/zoom.

Scope
- Measure and maintain consistent cell width/height across DPR changes.
- Fit-to-container logic (like xterm fit addon) with debounced resize.
- User-controlled zoom/font size with deterministic mapping to cell metrics.

Testing
- Deterministic tests for geometry math (given font metrics + DPR -> cell size).
- E2E: resize storm in browser emits JSONL with geometry snapshots + frame hashes.
